title: Ceanothus × regius
intro: Ceanothus × regius, also known as California lilac or bluebottle, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the western United States, characterized by its fragrant blue flowers and attractive foliage. The leaves and flowers of Ceanothus × regius have been used in herbal medicine for their potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, such as ceanothine and ceanothic acid, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ceanothus × regius is commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into traditional folk remedies and modern herbal supplements.
taxonomy: Ceanothus × regius is a hybrid of the Ceanothus species, specifically a cross between Ceanothus arizonicus and Ceanothus californicus. It is a member of the Rhamnaceae family, which includes buckthorns and other related plants. This classification places Ceanothus × regius within the order Rosales and the clade asterids. The genus Ceanothus is native to the western parts of North America, where Ceanothus × regius has been cultivated for its ornamental value. As a hybrid, Ceanothus × regius is not strictly a species in its own right but rather a distinct cultivar.
uses: Ceanothus × regius is known for its potential medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Its leaves and stems contain compounds that may have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. However, the specific uses and benefits of Ceanothus × regius require more scientific study to determine their validity. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any plant for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Ceanothus × regius, also known as the Herbert's California lilac, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and may help alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's leaves and stems are said to possess antiseptic qualities, which could aid in wound healing and preventing infections. Additionally, Ceanothus × regius has been studied for its antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. More research is needed to fully understand the extent of its health benefits.
properties: Ceanothus × regius is a hybrid species known for its potential therapeutic properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its benefits. Some studies suggest that compounds found in Ceanothus species may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions. constituents: Ceanothus × regius contains active constituents such as flavonoids, including ceanothin, kaempferol, and quercetin. These flavonoids are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Ceanothus × regius also contains triterpenoid saponins, which have been reported to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-cancer activities. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The active constituents in Ceanothus × regius have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and skin issues.
parts: Ceanothus × regius is also known as the California lilac or bluebells, and its medicinal parts are primarily the leaves, stems, and roots. The plant contains various alkaloids, including ceanothine and ceanothidine, which have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties. The leaves and stems of Ceanothus × regius have been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, while the roots have been used to treat digestive issues and as a diuretic. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its ability to inhibit inflammation and kill bacteria. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and its safety for human consumption.
preparations: Ceanothus × regius is a hybrid of the California lilac, known for its fragrant flowers and potential medicinal properties.
